permissionsPanel.codexSandboxDesc: Control system access boundaries when Codex executes commands. Defaults to danger-full-access. Switch to workspace-write for stricter sandboxing.

permissionsPanel.fullAccessDesc: Allows higher system access, suitable for commands like adb or docker that require extra permissions.
permissionsPanel.hint: This setting only affects Codex process sandboxing and does not change Claude Code permission behavior.

provider.localProviderDisableMessage: This will stop using ~/.claude/settings.json and leave Claude with no active provider until you explicitly enable another one.
provider.localProviderHelp:
When using local provider mode:
• The plugin will not modify your ~/.claude/settings.json file
• You need to manually manage your configurations
• Make sure the file exists and contains valid JSON
• Suitable for advanced users who prefer manual control
provider.cliLoginProviderDescription: Reuse your existing Claude CLI login state completed in the terminal
provider.cliLoginAuthorizeMessage: This will allow the plugin to use your Claude CLI login session for API requests. You must have already logged in via the Claude CLI in your terminal.
provider.cliLoginAuthorizeDetail: The plugin delegates authentication to the Claude SDK's native OAuth flow. You can revoke this authorization at any time.
provider.cliLoginDisableMessage: This will stop using CLI login state and leave Claude with no active provider until you explicitly enable another one.

subscriptionTutorial.dialogTitle: How to use a subscription account

claude.title: How to use Claude Code Subscription Account
claude.item1: Log in to your Claude Code subscription account via claude code CLI in the terminal, then you can chat normally in the terminal
claude.item2: Make sure the ~/.claude/settings.json file and .env file are empty
claude.item3: Restart IDEA, and you can chat normally
claude.note1: If you have done the above but the plugin still does not respond, try setting ~/.claude/settings.json .env to:
claude.issuePrefix: If it still doesn't work, please report an issue on GitHub:

codex.title: Codex Subscription Account Setup
codex.item1: Run codex login in the terminal Codex CLI and log in successfully, then you can chat in the terminal

skills.title: Skills
skills.importHint: Click + button to import Skill file or folder
skills.deleteMessage:
Are you sure you want to delete {{scope}} Skill "{{name}}"?

This action cannot be undone.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Skills are dynamically loaded instruction sets, scripts, and resource folders that enhance Claude's performance for specific tasks. Skills teach Claude how to complete particular tasks in a repeatable way, such as creating documents using company brand guidelines, analyzing data following organization-specific workflows, or automating personal tasks.

structure.title: Skill Structure
structure.description: A Skill is a folder containing a SKILL.md file:
structure.example:
my-skill/
├── SKILL.md          # Required: skill definition file
├── templates/        # Optional: template files
└── references/       # Optional: reference materials

format.title: SKILL.md Format
format.description: SKILL.md files use YAML frontmatter + Markdown format:
format.example:
---
name: my-skill-name
description: Skill description and when to use
---

# Skill Instructions

Detailed instruction content...
format.hint: name and description are required fields. Optional fields include license, allowed-tools, metadata

tips.title: Usage Tips
tips.item1: Ensure Skill directory contains a valid SKILL.md file
tips.item2: Skill names must use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens (hyphen-case)
tips.item3: Skills automatically take effect in Claude sessions after loading
tips.item4: Control individual Skill status with enable/disable toggles
tips.item5: Mention Skill name in chat to use it, e.g., "Use the pdf skill to extract form fields"

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Codex Skills are instruction sets stored in .agents/skills/ directories that enhance Codex's performance for specific tasks. Invoke them in chat using the $ prefix.

structure.title: Skill Structure
structure.description: A Codex Skill is a folder containing a SKILL.md file:
structure.example:
.agents/skills/
└── my-skill/
    ├── SKILL.md          # Required: skill definition file
    ├── templates/        # Optional: template files
    └── references/       # Optional: reference materials

format.title: SKILL.md Format
format.description: SKILL.md files use YAML frontmatter + Markdown format:
format.example:
---
name: my-skill-name
description: Skill description and when to use
---

# Skill Instructions

Detailed instruction content...
format.hint: name and description are required fields

tips.title: Usage Tips
tips.item1: Ensure Skill directory contains a valid SKILL.md file
tips.item2: Skill names must use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens
tips.item3: Type $ prefix in chat to trigger Skill autocomplete
tips.item4: Control individual Skill status with enable/disable toggles

rewind.title: Rewind Files to Previous State
rewind.tooltipFull: Restore files to their state at this message
rewind.noRewindableMessages: No rewindable messages in this session
rewind.willRestore: Will restore files to their state at this message
rewind.changesLost: Changes made after this point will be lost
rewind.notAvailable: Rewind not available for this session

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.description: The application encountered an unexpected error. Please copy the error details below and share them with the developer for troubleshooting.
errorBoundary.cause1: Node.js is not installed or not configured correctly
errorBoundary.helpText: If the problem persists, please copy the error information and submit it as an issue on GitHub, or share it in the community group.

default.label: Suggest (approval)
default.tooltip: Codex approval_policy=untrusted – prompts before editing files or running shell commands.
default.description: Safest option. Every write or command requires your approval.

plan.label: Plan Mode
plan.description: Uses only read-only tools, generates plan for user approval

acceptEdits.label: Auto Edit
acceptEdits.tooltip: Codex approval_policy=on-request – asks for approval when needed
acceptEdits.description: Balanced option. Allows normal editing flow while still requiring approvals when needed.

bypassPermissions.label: Full Auto
bypassPermissions.tooltip: Codex approval_policy=never – runs commands and writes files without prompting (workspace sandbox still enforced).
bypassPermissions.description: Hands-off mode. Codex executes edits and shell commands immediately.

gpt53codex.label: GPT-5.3-Codex
gpt53codex.description: Latest frontier agentic coding model with enhanced capabilities.

gpt55.label: GPT-5.5
gpt55.description: Latest frontier model with stronger capabilities.

gpt54.label: GPT-5.4
gpt54.description: Latest frontier model with enhanced capabilities.

gpt51codexMax.label: GPT-5.1-Codex-Max
gpt51codexMax.description: Codex-optimized flagship for deep and fast reasoning.

gpt52.label: GPT-5.2
gpt52.description: Optimized for professional work and long-running agents.

gpt51codexMini.label: GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini
gpt51codexMini.description: Optimized for Codex. Cheaper, faster, but less capable.

default.label: Default Mode
default.description: Requires manual confirmation for each operation, suitable for cautious use

plan.label: Plan Mode
plan.description: Uses only read-only tools, generates plan for user approval

acceptEdits.label: Agent Mode
acceptEdits.description: Automatically accept file creation/editing, reducing confirmation steps

bypassPermissions.label: Auto Mode
bypassPermissions.description: Fully automated, bypassing all permission checks [Use with caution]

error.providerNotConfiguredDesc: No AI provider is configured yet, or access to the local configuration has not been authorized. Please complete setup in Provider Management settings before starting a conversation.

codexThreadResume.title: Session thread is no longer valid
codexThreadResume.intro: Creating a new session will resolve this issue. The thread ID for the current session no longer exists on the Codex server.
codexThreadResume.reason: Codex clears old threads after a period of time, which can trigger this error when resuming a previous session.

sdkNativeBinaryMissing.title: SDK installation incomplete — native binary missing
sdkNativeBinaryMissing.intro: This is a known issue with the Taobao npm mirror (not a plugin bug). The mirror hasn't fully synchronized the platform-specific files. You can resolve it manually with either of the options below:
sdkNativeBinaryMissing.reason: This issue will disappear automatically once the Taobao mirror finishes syncing — no further action needed.

switchRegistry.title: Option 1: Temporarily switch to the official registry and reinstall
switchRegistry.step0: 1. Run this in your terminal (switch to the official npm registry):
switchRegistry.step1: 2. Open the plugin's Dependency Management settings and click Reinstall:
switchRegistry.step2: 3. After the reinstall completes, switch back to the Taobao mirror:

downgrade.title: Option 2: Roll back to an older version
downgrade.step0: In Dependency Management, switch the SDK version to v0.2.112 or earlier. Restart IDEA after switching.

spawnEbusy.title: spawn EBUSY — child process failed to launch
spawnEbusy.intro: This error is typically caused by an incompatible Node.js version or an incomplete SDK installation. Try the two options below in order:
spawnEbusy.reason: EBUSY is a system error raised by Node.js when a child process can't be launched due to a busy resource. Common causes: Node.js 24 compatibility issues, an outdated SDK, or an incomplete install from the Taobao mirror.

checkNodeVersion.title: Option 1: Check your Node.js version
checkNodeVersion.step0: Run the command below in a terminal to see your version. Node 22 or the current LTS is recommended — users have reported many issues on Node 24, so prefer Node 20 or 22:

reinstallLatestSdk.title: Option 2: Reinstall the latest SDK from the official npm registry
reinstallLatestSdk.step0: 1. Switch to the official npm registry (avoid the Taobao mirror — it may not have the latest version):
reinstallLatestSdk.step1: 2. Open Dependency Management and reinstall the latest SDK:
